what_it_does: A self-contained structural regression/acceptance test for runtime/cc_clone.py. It mocks the supervisor and materializer, then exercises operator_launch_cmd, clone registry I/O, _find_clone, end_clone, list_clones, and the safety-boundary guarantees that prepare_at is materialize-only while clone_at spawns a supervised clone. It also tests model/fallback passthrough, provider passthrough, and context-size-aware Ollama model selection.
observations: Uses a global PASS/FAIL list and an inline ok() helper instead of a framework like pytest.
Monkeypatches cc_clone functions and constants (_sup, materialize_at_point, resume_cwd_for, build_timeline, _wait_idle, CLONES_DIR, CHAN_DIR) to keep the test hermetic.
Verifies the exact command string the operator must run, including --resume, --mcp-config channel.mcp.json, --dangerously-load-development-channels, env vars, and cwd.
Tests registry lookup by handle, session_id, and supervisor_session, and fail-loud behavior for unknown clones.
Tests end_clone teardown, list_clones pruning of dead supervised sessions, and exact channel-member schema registration/deregistration.
Contains explicit safety-boundary assertions: prepare_at must never call the supervisor, clone_at must call /spawn.

Tests recent passthrough features: model/fallback into /spawn body, provider into /spawn body, and context-size-aware Ollama model picking (kimi default vs deepseek-v4-flash for large/failed contexts).
standouts: The safety-boundary guarantee tests: a forbidden supervisor spy for prepare_at and a required /spawn spy for clone_at.
The exact channel-member schema assertion with set equality of keys and transport=supervised.
The context-size-aware Ollama model picker tests with small, big, explicit non-kimi, and missing-file cases.
conventions: Inline test runner with global PASS/FAIL state.
Heavy monkeypatching of the module under test rather than dependency injection.
Tests use internal/private functions and constants (e.g. _find_clone, _sup, KIMI_OLLAMA_MODEL).
Cleans up with shutil.rmtree at the end.
Comments include dates/TIM RULE markers tying tests to recent changes.
concerns: Monkeypatching module globals can leak state between test sections within the same process.
Relies on the internal shape of cc_clone implementation, making the test brittle to refactors.
Some assertions depend on exact key sets and command strings that may change as the wrapper evolves.
Context-size estimation is only indirectly exercised; the big-file test uses a single oversized text entry rather than realistic message history.
notes: A mock-heavy acceptance gate that encodes the prepare/clone safety boundary and recent model/provider/context-size wiring as executable assertions.
questions: What does the real runtime/cc_clone.py implementation look like beyond the tested surface?
How does _pick_ollama_model estimate context size from a .jsonl conversation file?
What is the semantics of compact:1 and other at-point identifiers?
How is the supervisor base URL configured and what does the channel.mcp.json contain?
What happens if the monkeypatched _sup spy is not restored correctly between test sections?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a structural regression gate for the cc_clone wrapper that uses mocks to prove wrapper logic and the prepare/clone safety boundary.
apparent_themes: Safety boundary between operator-gated and autonomous clone paths
intention_signals: The file is trying to freeze the contract of cc_clone: that prepare_at stays materialize-only, clone_at always spawns a supervised session, and that recent model/fallback/provider/context-size wiring reaches the supervisor /spawn body unchanged.
novelty: The context-size-aware Ollama model picker tests and the explicit 'byte-identical default' provider omission test are distinctive; the safety-boundary assertion style (forbidden spy vs required spy) is also notable.
contribution: A runnable, file-local acceptance harness that documents and guards the prepare/clone safety boundary, registry lifecycle, channel-member schema, and model/provider/context-size passthrough contracts for the cc_clone wrapper.
summary_for_embedding: A self-contained acceptance test for runtime/cc_clone.py that mocks the supervisor and materializer to verify the prepare/clone safety boundary, registry lifecycle, channel-member schema, and model/provider/context-size passthroughs.
intention_for_embedding: To regression-guard the wrapper logic around point-in-time clones, ensuring operator-gated prepare never spawns while autonomous clone spawns, and that recent model/provider/context-size wiring reaches the supervisor spawn body.

symbols.does: Inline assertion helper that records pass/fail in global PASS/FAIL lists and prints the result.
symbols.description: The ok function is the tiny test framework for this file: it evaluates a boolean condition, appends the test name to PASS or FAIL, prints ok/FAIL, and optionally prints a detail message on failure.

symbols.does: A supervisor spy that raises AssertionError if prepare_at tries to call the supervisor.
symbols.description: This lambda is assigned to cc_clone._sup during the prepare_at safety-boundary test. If prepare_at attempts any supervisor interaction, the test fails loudly, proving the operator-gated path is materialize-only.

symbols.does: A supervisor spy that records when /spawn is called and returns a fake session.
symbols.description: Assigned to cc_clone._sup during the clone_at safety-boundary test, this function sets spawned['called'] = True on a /spawn request and returns a mock supervisor session, allowing the test to verify that clone_at spawns a supervised clone.

symbols.does: A supervisor spy that captures the /spawn request body to inspect model and fallback_model.
symbols.description: This function updates a global spawn_body dictionary with the body of any /spawn request, then returns a mock session. It is used to verify the Fable-era resume fix passes model and fallback_model verbatim into the spawn body.

symbols.does: A supervisor spy that captures the /spawn request body to inspect provider and resolved model.
symbols.description: Similar to _spy_body, but focused on the provider passthrough test: it records provider and the resolved model in the spawn body for an Ollama-native clone, confirming the provider field reaches the supervisor.

symbols.does: A supervisor spy that captures the /spawn request body to confirm provider is omitted by default.
symbols.description: This spy records the /spawn body when no provider is supplied, enabling the assertion that the default Anthropic path remains byte-identical and contains no provider key.
